= Chapter 4: UI/UX Architecture and Design System

== 4.1 UX Operating Model

Kosha's UI architecture is not only visual styling. It is an interaction contract: each page must provide quick orientation, a clear next action, and stable trust signals while data is loading or mutating.

Primary product intent and UX constraints are codified in the playbook:

. Product intent and page purpose map are defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L3[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md] and link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L10[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].
. Card anatomy, spacing rhythm, and status semantics are defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L74[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md], link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L76[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md], link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L83[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md], and link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L88[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].
. Interaction safety rules are defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L94[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].
. First-viewport clarity and action linkage are explicit quality gates in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L117[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].

Architectural implication:

. UI composition order matters as much as component style.
. Loading and mutation states are part of trust UX, not implementation detail.
. Motion must communicate state change, not just decorate transitions.

'''

== 4.2 Design Language: Tokens and Semantics

== 4.2.1 Color system and semantic intent

The design system uses semantic color tokens instead of page-local hardcoded colors.

Tailwind semantic palette is defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L6[tailwind.config.js]:

. Base surfaces: kosha-bg, kosha-surface, kosha-border.
. Domain semantics: income, expense, invest, warning.
. Ink hierarchy: ink, ink-2, ink-3, ink-4.
. Brand axis: deep charcoal plus warm gold accent.

Runtime CSS variables mirror this semantic model in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L59[src/index.css], including:

. Brand and accent variables.
. Status colors for income/expense/warning.
. Hero-card specific contrast variables.
. Shared shimmer colors for loading surfaces.

This dual-token strategy means:

. Utility classes and handcrafted CSS stay visually aligned.
. Theme refinements can be done centrally without rewriting page JSX.
. Charts, cards, sheets, and nav surfaces preserve one semantic language.

== 4.2.2 Typography scale and numeric readability

Typography scales are defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L71[tailwind.config.js], with explicit tiers:

. caption and label for metadata.
. body for dense transactional text.
. value, display, and hero for financial emphasis.

Key implementation details:

. Base font family is set globally in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L32[src/index.css].
. Currency-heavy surfaces use tabular-nums patterns throughout major pages (for example in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L899[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Transactions.jsx#L374[src/pages/Transactions.jsx], and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L356[src/pages/Monthly.jsx]).
. Playbook spacing/type rhythm is explicitly documented in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L83[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].

== 4.2.3 Shape, depth, and tactile hierarchy

Shape and depth are centralized in Tailwind tokens:

. Radius scale in link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L80[tailwind.config.js].
. Shadow scale in link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L88[tailwind.config.js].

Shared component primitives apply those tokens:

. Primary card primitive in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L145[src/index.css].
. List card primitive in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L218[src/index.css].
. Action chips and buttons in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L253[src/index.css], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L311[src/index.css], and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L338[src/index.css].

Practical result:

. Visual hierarchy is encoded by token tiers rather than ad hoc per-page decisions.
. Destructive, warning, and neutral actions stay consistently legible.

'''

== 4.3 Layout and Composition System

== 4.3.1 Page shell and safe-area contract

The global page shell is safe-area aware and mobile-first:

. Safe-area and nav-height variables are defined in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L61[src/index.css].
. Main page wrapper geometry is in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L524[src/index.css].
. Frosted sticky page header is implemented by link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/layout/PageHeader.jsx#L3[src/components/layout/PageHeader.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L194[src/index.css].

The same shell scales to desktop with wider max width and altered spacing in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L692[src/index.css].

== 4.3.2 The practical 60/40 ergonomic split

Kosha does not encode a literal CSS ratio named "60/40". Instead, it implements a consistent attention split through composition order:

. Top-of-viewport surfaces prioritize orientation and action.
. Lower surfaces expand diagnostics and historical detail.

This is aligned with the first-viewport quality gate in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L118[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md] and visible in page structure:

. Dashboard first renders greeting, hero, and nudges before deeper analytics in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L809[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L822[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx], and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L839[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].
. Monthly first renders hero and month-close summary before deeper breakdown surfaces in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L327[src/pages/Monthly.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L349[src/pages/Monthly.jsx].

So the 60/40 principle is an information hierarchy rule, not a hardcoded grid ratio.

== 4.3.3 Navigation architecture

Desktop and mobile navigation intentionally diverge by ergonomics:

. Desktop uses a fixed frosted sidebar in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L383[src/App.jsx].
. Mobile uses a floating glass dock in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L458[src/App.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L378[src/index.css].
. Mobile nav hides on downward scroll via link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.js#L13[src/hooks/useScrollDirection.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L476[src/App.jsx].
. Nav prefetches route chunks/data on hover, focus, and touch-start through link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L94[src/App.jsx].

This separation reduces thumb travel on mobile while preserving high information density on desktop.

== 4.3.4 Sheet and dialog system

Overlay primitives use a consistent bottom-sheet model:

. Shared backdrop/panel geometry in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L479[src/index.css] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L486[src/index.css].
. Transaction sheet orchestration in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L360[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx].
. Budget management sheet using Radix dialog portal in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/categories/BudgetSheet.jsx#L98[src/components/categories/BudgetSheet.jsx].
. Destructive confirmation dialog with the same motion grammar in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/dialogs/DeleteDialog.jsx#L11[src/components/dialogs/DeleteDialog.jsx].

This keeps modal interactions predictable while allowing different semantic intensities (data entry vs destructive confirmation).

'''

== 4.4 Motion System (Framer Motion + CSS)

== 4.4.1 Shared motion constants and easing

Core motion constants are centralized in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/animations.js#L2[src/lib/animations.js]:

. Shared ease curve: [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1].
. Fade-up and stagger builders in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/animations.js#L4[src/lib/animations.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/animations.js#L18[src/lib/animations.js].
. Spring presets for premium and gentle interactions in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/animations.js#L26[src/lib/animations.js].

CSS also defines a motion timing scale in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L100[src/index.css].

== 4.4.2 Route transition choreography

Route transitions are explicit and stateful:

. Page transition object in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L70[src/App.jsx].
. AnimatePresence wait mode for exit-before-enter behavior in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L810[src/App.jsx].
. Route content keyed by pathname in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L811[src/App.jsx].

This avoids abrupt route swaps and preserves navigational continuity.

== 4.4.3 In-page reveal and progressive emphasis

Dashboard uses fade-up plus stagger for a progressive reveal sequence:

. Motion builders imported in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L18[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].
. Fade/stagger instances in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L45[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].
. Sequential section rendering in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L801[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].

The same principle appears in filter panels and month-change transitions:

. Category filter reveal in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Transactions.jsx#L487[src/pages/Transactions.jsx].
. Month switch transition in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L327[src/pages/Monthly.jsx].

== 4.4.4 Interaction physics for navigation and overlays

Motion values are chosen to feel dense but controlled:

. Mobile nav tap spring in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L489[src/App.jsx].
. Active-pill layout animation in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L493[src/App.jsx].
. Sheet enter springs around stiffness 400 with tuned damping in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L368[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/categories/BudgetSheet.jsx#L117[src/components/categories/BudgetSheet.jsx], and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/dialogs/DeleteDialog.jsx#L27[src/components/dialogs/DeleteDialog.jsx].

== 4.4.5 Reduced-motion compliance

A global reduced-motion media rule is enforced in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L575[src/index.css], collapsing animation and transition duration for accessibility-sensitive users.

'''

== 4.5 Loading-State Architecture and Anti-Flash Strategy

Kosha distinguishes initial blocking load from background freshness updates. This is the most important UX trust pattern in the app.

== 4.5.1 Route and auth loading

Route-level loading has two coordinated layers:

. Suspense fallback wrapper in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L62[src/App.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L64[src/App.jsx].
. Auth guard skeleton-by-route while auth/profile resolve in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L147[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx#L176[src/components/navigation/AuthGuard.jsx].

This prevents blank-route flashes during auth bootstrap and redirect transitions.

== 4.5.2 Initial load vs background fetch in data hooks

Hooks expose both loading and fetching where needed:

. Month summary returns both in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L448[src/hooks/useTransactions.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L473[src/hooks/useTransactions.js].
. Running balance returns both in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L608[src/hooks/useTransactions.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L631[src/hooks/useTransactions.js].
. Recent transactions returns both in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L265[src/hooks/useTransactions.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L285[src/hooks/useTransactions.js].

Dashboard then composes these states explicitly:

. Initial hero blocking state in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L318[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].
. Non-blocking background sync state in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L319[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].
. Lightweight sync hint text without tearing content in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L816[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx].

== 4.5.3 Placeholder continuity (no flash on revalidation)

Hook-level placeholderData keeps previous data visible while fresh data is fetched:

. Transactions list in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L219[src/hooks/useTransactions.js].
. Month summary and running balance in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L470[src/hooks/useTransactions.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useTransactions.js#L628[src/hooks/useTransactions.js].
. Liabilities and loans in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useLiabilities.js#L56[src/hooks/useLiabilities.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useLoans.js#L60[src/hooks/useLoans.js].
. Budgets and user categories in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useBudgets.js#L26[src/hooks/useBudgets.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/hooks/useUserCategories.js#L91[src/hooks/useUserCategories.js].

This is the primary anti-flicker mechanism.

== 4.5.4 Skeleton system and staged heavy rendering

Skeleton architecture is shared and composable:

. Skeleton primitive component in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/common/SkeletonLayout.jsx#L1[src/components/common/SkeletonLayout.jsx].
. Shimmer keyframes and class in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L596[src/index.css] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L605[src/index.css].

Pages then use two-stage loading:

. Blocking skeleton for first render in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Transactions.jsx#L546[src/pages/Transactions.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L319[src/pages/Monthly.jsx].
. Deferred heavy surfaces after first paint via heavyReady in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L273[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx#L280[src/pages/Dashboard.jsx], link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L34[src/pages/Monthly.jsx], and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Monthly.jsx#L38[src/pages/Monthly.jsx].

== 4.5.5 Mutation progress and integrity UX

For write flows, UX is intentionally strict:

. Add transaction disables all controls immediately on submit in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L336[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx].
. Backdrop and close action are blocked while saving in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L363[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L383[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx].
. Sheet closes only after mutation success in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx#L344[src/components/transactions/AddTransactionSheet.jsx].

This implements the playbook rule to keep destructive or critical row-state changes explicit and trustworthy.

== 4.5.6 Realtime freshness without visual churn

Realtime invalidation is intentionally delayed and suppressed to prevent refetch storms:

. Debounced invalidation scheduler in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L549[src/App.jsx] with 300ms delay at link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L555[src/App.jsx].
. Mutation suppression guard checks before invalidation in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/App.jsx#L550[src/App.jsx].

UI implication:

. Users see stable optimistic state immediately.
. Realtime catches remote truth shortly after without visible thrash.

'''

== 4.6 Responsive and Device Ergonomics

Kosha is mobile-first and thumb-aware, then expands density for desktop.

Core mechanisms:

. Safe-area variables and nav height in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L61[src/index.css].
. Mobile-first page spacing and bottom inset in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L524[src/index.css].
. Small-device compaction at <=390px in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L611[src/index.css].
. Desktop page expansion at >=768px in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L692[src/index.css].
. Desktop sheet repositioning around sidebar geometry at link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L717[src/index.css].
. Header safe-top padding in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/layout/PageHeader.jsx#L8[src/components/layout/PageHeader.jsx].

This architecture keeps mobile interaction targets comfortable while preserving desktop information throughput.

'''

== 4.7 Implementation Rules for Future UI Work

When adding or revising UI, follow these guardrails:

. Use semantic tokens first, not page-local raw colors. Start from link:{repo-blob-url}/tailwind.config.js#L6[tailwind.config.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L59[src/index.css].
. Keep first viewport action-oriented per quality gates in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L118[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].
. Preserve the loading split:
   - isLoading for initial skeleton.
   - isFetching for background freshness indicators.
. Use placeholderData for data-heavy views where visual continuity matters.
. Keep sheet/dialog motion within existing spring ranges unless there is a measured UX reason to diverge.
. Respect reduced-motion behavior in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/index.css#L575[src/index.css].
. For destructive or write-critical actions, disable interactive exits until mutation resolves.

If these rules are followed, new surfaces remain consistent with Kosha's current trust-performance UX model.
